studies.risk_class: Risk Class IIa according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The intervention (DiGA Kaia Health) is received over 12 weeks, with exercises performed daily for 10-30 minutes.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention group: DiGA Kaia Health; Control group: standard prevention for back pain.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Intervention group: Subjects receive the DiGA Kaia Health over 12 weeks and should perform exercises daily for between 10-30 minutes. Control group: Receives standard prevention for back pain.
studies.collected_data: Physical function (IPAQ, HAQ), quality of life (SF-36), sleep behavior (PSQI), depressive moods (BDI), movement and health competence (PAHCO), gait pattern (Gait analysis with sensoric), mobility (BASMI), and compliance (Compliance Questionnaire).

studies.risk_class: Risk Class IIa according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: The primary endpoint is assessed at day 90 (study end). Secondary assessments are conducted at 30 and 60 days. The study was prematurely terminated.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention group: Usual care + Kaia Back Pain app. Control group: Usual care.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: The intervention group used the Kaia Back Pain app in addition to their standard care for 90 days. The control group received standard care but was given access to the Kaia Back Pain app at the end of the study.
studies.collected_data: Oswestry Disability Index (ODI) version 2.1b for physical function; Short Form Health Survey 12 (SF12) for health-related quality of life; 11-point Numeric Rating Scale (NRS) for pain intensity (average pain in the previous week); Depression-Anxiety-Stress-Scale (DASS) for depression, anxiety, and stress. Responder rates (partial and complete response based on NRS, and 30% or greater reduction in ODI) were also tracked.

studies.risk_class: Risk Class IIa according to MDR
studies.duration_additional_info: Follow-ups are conducted at 6, 12, and 24 weeks. The total trial period is 6 months.
studies.intervention_and_control_group: Intervention group: kaia LBP app. Control group: standard physical therapy and online education.
studies.additional_info_about_trial_arms: Arm 1 (Intervention): Treatment by kaia LBP app throughout the whole trial period of 6 months, self-administered on own mobile device, encouraged to use at least four times a week. Arm 2 (Control): Standard physical therapy once a week for 6 weeks plus weekly emails with links to online education resources. After week 12, control patients are granted access to the kaia app until the trial ends after 6 months.
studies.collected_data: Pain index (assessed on an 11-point numeric rating scale [NRS] for current, maximum, and average pain over the last 4 weeks), functional capability (using HFAQ), health-related quality of life (using VR-12), Graded Chronic Pain Scale (GCPS), and pain self-efficacy (pain self-efficacy questionnaire). Assessed at enrollment, and at 6-, 12-, and 24-week follow-ups.

audit.study_title: Randomized controlled trial to evaluate the effectiveness of a digital health application (Kaia) to prevent back pain and improve quality of life in hospital staff
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: Kaia back pain for the treatment of non-specific low back pain in adult patients - a randomised controlled trial
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results

audit.study_title: e-Health and mobile health application for the self-management support of patients with low back pain - a confirmatory trial
audit.note: registry-only: outcome measures captured under collected_data; no reported results
